description: Institution-level cost, earnings, debt, and outcomes data for degree programs across U.S. higher education institutions

unit_id.description: Unique institution identifier in College Scorecard database

admission_rate.description: Share of applicants admitted to the institution

average_sat.description: Average SAT score of enrolled students

cost_of_attendance.description: Average annual cost in dollars including tuition, fees, room, board, books, and supplies

pell_share.description: Share of students receiving Pell Grants

completion_rate.description: Share of students who graduate or transfer within 150% of normal time

retention_rate.description: Share of first-time, full-time students who return for a second year

repayment_rate_3yr.description: Share of borrowers who have repaid at least $1 within 3 years of entering repayment

median_debt.description: Median cumulative debt of borrowers who left the institution

median_earnings_10yr.description: Median earnings of students 10 years after entry to the institution

median_earnings_monthly.description: Median monthly earnings 10 years after entry

share_earning_above_25k.description: Share of students earning above $25,000 ten years after entry

earnings_to_cost_ratio.description: Ratio of median 10-year earnings to cost of attendance (>1 means earnings exceed cost)

debt_to_earnings_ratio.description: Ratio of median debt to median 10-year earnings (<0.5 is favorable)

roi_value_score.description: Composite score reflecting overall value (cost-adjusted earnings, debt burden, completion, repayment)

roi_value_score.interpretation: Higher scores indicate better value. Score = (earnings_to_cost_ratio × 30) + (completion_rate × 30) + ((1 - debt_to_earnings_ratio) × 25) + (repayment_rate × 15)

earnings_to_cost_ratio.interpretation: Ratio of 1+ means 10-year earnings exceed cost of attendance. Higher is better.

debt_to_earnings_ratio.interpretation: Lower is better. <0.5 means debt is manageable relative to earnings.

completion_rate.interpretation: Percentage of students who graduate or transfer within 150% of normal time

data_quality_notes: Some institutions may have missing values in specialized fields (SAT, debt to earnings ratio)
Data represents most recent cohort available in College Scorecard
Earnings data is sample-based and may not represent all graduates
Cost of attendance includes living expenses and may vary by program within an institution

dashboard_tabs.Executive Summary: Overview of ROI score distribution and relationship between cost and earnings across all institutions
dashboard_tabs.Value Map: Breakdown of average ROI value scores by degree level and institution type (public/private/for-profit)
dashboard_tabs.Cost vs Earnings: Scatter plot and distributions showing relationship between cost of attendance and 10-year median earnings by region
dashboard_tabs.Debt & Completion: Analysis of student debt, completion rates, and 3-year loan repayment rates by degree and institution type
dashboard_tabs.Institution Drilldown: Filter and compare top-performing institutions by ROI value score within selected region or institution type

intended_use: Prospective students comparing colleges by financial value and outcomes
Financial aid officers understanding institutional cost-effectiveness
Policy makers evaluating higher education value and affordability
